stance: Every script here is a current working answer to a recurring need. All are explicitly open to replacement by something better.

paper_engine.py.purpose: Daily-close paper-trade ledger for armed theses
open_questions: Daily-close MTM is a crude proxy for intraday strategies; the attention_fade_rddt thesis is intraday but tracked daily
No multi-leg support yet — pair trades and spreads have to be opened as two positions
Borrow cost is hardcoded; should pull live or recent FINRA short-interest data
No corporate-action handling (splits, dividends)
candidate_successors: A multi-leg ledger with intraday MTM via 5-min bars
Public.com API integration via existing skill for real fill data once G1 is cleared

open_questions: Action priority is hardcoded; should it be learned from outcome data?
Currently action-budget-bounded but doesn't account for cost — a SEED might be cheaper than ITERATE_STALE for the same expected gain

run_iteration.py.purpose: Scaffold a new PR file from the template
open_questions: Scaffolds a stub the meta-orchestrator can mistake for an iteration (caused first HPR false-positive)
Should perhaps emit a sentinel file rather than a draft PR until the iteration agent actually fills it

hypothesis_space.py.purpose: Thompson-sampling generator over (signal × universe × horizon × structure)
open_questions: Independent sampling per axis assumes axes are independent — they aren't (intraday horizon ↔ short-only structure correlate)
Beta priors are uninformed; bootstrap from prior session learnings?
No mechanism yet to incorporate explicit user preferences into the prior
Doesn't yet run cross-sectional generalization automatically after single-name survival
Naming scheme creates noisy directory listings

multiple_testing.py.purpose: BH FDR correction over weekly hypothesis families
open_questions: Family = week is arbitrary; should family be by signal-family or by exploration session?
Doesn't account for correlation between hypotheses (BH-Y would be more honest)
Hash-based holdout may correlate accidentally with calendar features
30% is a guess — should be tuned to the actual size of the typical universe

run_evals.py.purpose: Deterministic eval suite over runs/ artifacts
open_questions: Capability evals are mostly counts; should be effect-size weighted
Spearman in E-CAP-01 is computed in pure Python — fine for n<100, slow beyond that
Template-stub detection is a string-match hack; brittle to template changes

meta_orchestrator.py.purpose: Reasoning layer over harness; drafts HPR stubs
open_questions: EVAL_TO_COMPONENT mapping is hand-coded; should be learned from which HPRs actually improved which evals
No mechanism for meta-orchestrator to propose changes to itself (recursive bound)

self_improve.py.purpose: GEPA-style reflective HPR drafter (currently a simpler version)
open_questions: Not yet wired into the cron — meta_orchestrator.py supersedes parts of it
Should either merge with meta_orchestrator.py or be deprecated
Currently emits a prompt; doesn't yet spawn the subagent itself
No tracking of critic↔primary divergence over time

iterate.py.purpose: Thin I/O wrapper for thesis iterations: prepare (lock + packet) → subagent does work → finalize (status, bandit, p-value, paper-open, release).
open_questions: finalize ticker inference falls back to thesis_id.split last token — brittle; should read from thesis.md or signal axis
No retry on partial failure (e.g. if paper_engine.open fails after status change)
Subagent spawning is done by the cron itself, not by iterate.py — could centralize
Hash on tick start could collide if two ticks fire in same millisecond — unlikely but possible

notify_budget.py.purpose: Daily notification rate limiting + coalesced digest
open_questions: Cap of 6 high/day is a guess — should be tuned to user preference

continuous.py.purpose: Session-resident background process firing cheap ticks every N seconds
open_questions: Daemonization via fork is fragile in some envs; would prefer a proper process manager

backtest_runner.py.purpose: Fully shell-executable thesis iteration: prepare → backtest → 3 falsifiers → PR write → finalize → release. No agent-layer dependency.
open_questions: Only runs 3 of 11 falsifiers (random-label, date-split, cost-stress) — needs ext. for the full playbook
Generic long/short basket model; no thesis-specific signal logic (event-conditional, attention-spike, factor-neutral)
HUMAN_THESIS_DEFAULTS is hand-coded; should be auto-extracted from thesis.md
Universe ticker resolution for grammar-generated theses uses a placeholder default
No support yet for intraday strategies (e.g. attention_fade_rddt is daily-close-to-close here)

data_fetcher.py.purpose: Persistent yfinance + FRED + earnings cache with 24h freshness check; warm-pack of ~80 tickers + 10 macro series
open_questions: No options surface data
No CDS / credit-tranche data
24h cache freshness is a guess — should be per-source

historical_sweep.py.purpose: Event-study runner across an event calendar × ticker universe, with BH FDR correction
open_questions: Two-sided t-test only — no event-type-specific direction
Event-window is symmetric; some catalysts have asymmetric impact
BH applied within sweep only; not joint with the rest of htest_log

frontier.py.purpose: Compute current (signal,universe,horizon,structure) frontier from bandit arm posteriors + STATUS files
open_questions: Single-axis posteriors only — no joint distribution over the 4-tuple
history quality varies; corporate actions are messy
coverage gaps on intraday macro
dependent on pplx CLI availability
urllib3 compatibility, rate limits — needs replacement
no historical API; need to poll + accumulate
free, full history available — high value
Earnings calendars per-ticker still need full pre-fetch
NFP / PCE / ISM not yet captured
Need a generator that pulls these from FRED + Investing.com programmatically

wishlist: Generalized data-fetcher abstraction so adding a new alt-data source is a 5-line PR
A backtest primitive that handles cross-sectional ranking + neutralization cleanly
Live regime detector (vol, growth, inflation quadrant) that all theses can condition on
Position-sizing primitive that supports Kelly-fraction + risk parity + vol target without re-implementing each time
A 'thesis lineage' viewer showing how each new thesis evolved from prior corpses + merges
